items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

image.altText: Images from Easter egg hunt.

items.title: 'Sleep tight baby': Jurors in NT murder trial to see 'confronting' footage

children.content: Jurors in the trial for the alleged 2024 murder of Yiel Gatluak in Alice Springs have heard they will see "a degree of violence most of you probably won't be accustomed to".

image.altText: Close up of a photo of a young Sudanese man, in a suit and tie, white shirt, phone case, hands holding it.

items.title: Kumanjayi White's custody death investigation moves incrementally one year on

children.content: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

items.title: Population of NT town doubles overnight as flood evacuees arrive

children.content: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

image.altText: An aerial image of a small town.

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

image.altText: A kitchen full of floodwater.

items.title: Vision loss and self-harm — government warned of custody health risks

children.content: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

image.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

items.title: Broken toilet, emails down: The first 24 hours in space on Artemis II

image.altText: Four astronauts pose for a photo inside a spacecraft

children.content: The Orion capsule carrying four astronauts in NASA's Artemis II mission successfully fired its engines this morning, kicking the crew out of Earth's orbit and towards the moon.
